shortDescription: IRCC Express Entry · FSW/CEC/FST · CRS score · ITA

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current Express Entry profile fee and PR application fee on canada.ca. CRS cutoffs change each draw—never promise ITA.

alert.title: CRS score drives selection
alert.body: Express Entry is points-based. No ITA without meeting draw cutoff. Counsel on realistic CRS before taking PR application fees.

about.label: Description
about.value: Federal economic immigration system managing FSW, CEC, and FST programs. Candidates create profile, receive CRS score, and apply for PR if invited (ITA).

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Meet FSW/CEC/FST criteria · Valid language tests · ECA for foreign education · Sufficient CRS score · Medical and police clearances

about.label: ITA not guaranteed
about.value: Profile in pool does not guarantee invitation. CRS cutoffs vary per draw. PNP can add 600 points.

about.label: After approval
about.value: COPR issued; complete landing; apply for PR card. Meet residency obligations for citizenship pathway.

eligibility.criterion: CRS score competitive for recent draws
Proof of funds (FSW/ FST if applicable)
Medical and police completed after ITA

redFlagsBanner: Without ITA, do not submit PR application. Improve CRS via language retake, ECA, PNP, or Canadian experience before expecting invitation.

redFlags.title: CRS below recent draw cutoffs
redFlags.description: Client expects ITA with non-competitive score.
redFlags.fix: Retake language for higher CLB; pursue PNP; gain Canadian work experience

redFlags.title: Wrong NOC/TEER code
redFlags.description: Work experience coded incorrectly affecting points.
redFlags.fix: Verify TEER category against official duties and reference letters

redFlags.title: Reference letter deficiencies
redFlags.description: Employer letters missing duties, hours, or dates.
redFlags.fix: Detailed reference on company letterhead matching NOC duties

redFlags.title: ECA not completed
redFlags.description: Foreign degree not assessed before profile.

redFlags.title: Language scores expired
redFlags.description: IELTS/CELPIP older than 2 years at ITA.
redFlags.fix: Retake before accepting ITA if expiring

redFlags.title: Misrepresentation risk
redFlags.fix: Honest accurate profile—misrepresentation has permanent consequences

faqs.q: Can we guarantee ITA or PR?
faqs.a: No. CRS cutoffs change each draw. We improve profile quality but never promise ITA or approval.

faqs.q: What CRS score needed?
faqs.a: Varies each draw—check recent IRCC draw results. Category-based draws have different cutoffs.

faqs.q: How to improve CRS?
faqs.a: Higher language scores, additional education ECA, Canadian work experience, PNP nomination (+600), French language bonus.

faqs.q: What is PNP?
faqs.a: Provincial Nominee Program—province nomination adds 600 CRS points, virtually ensuring ITA.

faqs.q: Processing after ITA?
faqs.a: Typically 6 months standard for most EE applications—verify current IRCC processing times.

faqs.q: Can spouse be included?
faqs.a: Yes—spouse factors into CRS; include if beneficial to score.

faqs.q: Proof of funds when?
faqs.a: Required at ITA stage for FSW; amount per family size on canada.ca.

faqs.q: What if ITA declined?
faqs.a: Profile may re-enter pool if valid—assess options carefully.

faqs.q: What is Description for this service?
faqs.a: Federal economic immigration system managing FSW, CEC, and FST programs. Candidates create profile, receive CRS score, and apply for PR if invited (ITA).

faqs.q: What is Eligible applicants for this service?
faqs.a: Meet FSW/CEC/FST criteria · Valid language tests · ECA for foreign education · Sufficient CRS score · Medical and police clearances

faqs.q: What is ITA not guaranteed for this service?
faqs.a: Profile in pool does not guarantee invitation. CRS cutoffs vary per draw. PNP can add 600 points.

faqs.q: Important note on ITA not guaranteed?
faqs.a: Profile in pool does not guarantee invitation. CRS cutoffs vary per draw. PNP can add 600 points.

faqs.q: What is Key authority for this service?
faqs.a: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC)

faqs.q: What is After approval for this service?
faqs.a: COPR issued; complete landing; apply for PR card. Meet residency obligations for citizenship pathway.

faqs.q: Is Eligible program (FSW/CEC/FST) required?
faqs.a: Eligible program (FSW/CEC/FST) is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Language test (CLB minimum per program) required?
faqs.a: Language test (CLB minimum per program) is typically required.

faqs.q: Is ECA for foreign credentials (FSW) required?
faqs.a: WES/IQAS etc.

faqs.q: Is CRS score competitive for recent draws required?
faqs.a: Assess against recent cutoffs

faqs.q: Is Work experience documented (NOC/TEER) required?
faqs.a: Work experience documented (NOC/TEER) is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Proof of funds (FSW/ FST if applicable) required?
faqs.a: Proof of funds (FSW/ FST if applicable) is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Medical and police completed after ITA required?
faqs.a: Assess whether Medical and police completed after ITA is satisfied for this case.

faqs.q: What is the Processing time?
faqs.a: 6–12m — After ITA received

faqs.q: What is the Government fee?
faqs.a: CAD $1,525+ — PR application after ITA

faqs.q: What is the Our approval rate?
faqs.a: 94% — ~88% post-ITA benchmark

faqs.q: What is the Required docs?
faqs.a: 16 — + ECA, language, work ref

faqs.q: What is the Consultancy fee?
faqs.a: See fee tab — + govt & ECA/tests

faqs.q: What if the client has CRS below recent draw cutoffs?
faqs.a: Retake language for higher CLB; pursue PNP; gain Canadian work experience

faqs.q: Why is CRS below recent draw cutoffs a problem?
faqs.a: Client expects ITA with non-competitive score.

faqs.q: What if the client has Wrong NOC/TEER code?
faqs.a: Verify TEER category against official duties and reference letters

faqs.q: Why is Wrong NOC/TEER code a problem?
faqs.a: Work experience coded incorrectly affecting points.

compliance: Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Never guarantee visa or immigration approval; use approved-language only
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, government, and third-party costs
Paid RCIC representation requires valid CICC membership
proTips: Calculate CRS at intake with official calculator
Secure detailed employer reference letters early
PNP exploration if CRS below cutoff

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Never collect full PR fee before ITA—profile setup fee only until invitation received.

quiz.question: What is Express Entry?
options: An online system for managing certain economic PR applications
quiz.explanation: Express Entry manages profiles and applications for major federal economic programs.

quiz.question: Which federal programs are managed through Express Entry?
quiz.explanation: Express Entry includes Federal Skilled Worker, Canadian Experience Class, and Federal Skilled Trades.

quiz.question: What does CRS stand for?
quiz.explanation: CRS is the points system used to rank candidates.

quiz.question: What is an ITA?
quiz.explanation: An ITA invites a candidate to submit a complete PR application.

quiz.question: What does FSW stand for?
quiz.explanation: FSW is a federal economic program managed through Express Entry.

quiz.question: What does CEC stand for?
quiz.explanation: CEC is for candidates with qualifying Canadian skilled work experience.

quiz.question: What does FST stand for?
quiz.explanation: FST is the federal skilled trades program.

quiz.question: What can a provincial nomination do in Express Entry?
quiz.explanation: An enhanced nomination can add major CRS points and often leads to an ITA.

quiz.question: What is the Express Entry pool?
options: Eligible candidate profiles waiting for draws
quiz.explanation: Profiles in the pool are ranked against other candidates.

quiz.question: Which factors commonly affect CRS?
options: Age, education, language, and work experience
quiz.explanation: Core CRS factors include human capital and skill transferability criteria.

quiz.question: What is a language test used for?
quiz.explanation: Approved language results affect eligibility and CRS.

quiz.question: What is an ECA used for?
options: Comparing foreign education to Canadian standards
quiz.explanation: An ECA can be required for foreign education points and FSW eligibility.

quiz.question: What must exist before entering the pool?
options: Eligibility for at least one Express Entry program
quiz.explanation: A candidate must meet a managed program's criteria.

quiz.question: Who uses Express Entry?
quiz.explanation: Express Entry is focused on skilled economic permanent residence.

quiz.question: What happens after an ITA?
options: The candidate submits a complete electronic PR application by the deadline
quiz.explanation: An ITA starts the deadline for the full application and evidence.

quiz.question: What is an Express Entry draw?
options: An invitation round where IRCC invites selected candidates
quiz.explanation: IRCC conducts rounds based on CRS, program, category, or other criteria.

quiz.question: What is proof of funds used for?
quiz.explanation: Some applicants must show available funds to settle in Canada.

quiz.question: Which program is tied to Canadian skilled work experience?
quiz.explanation: CEC is designed for qualifying Canadian work experience.

quiz.question: What is a primary occupation?
options: The occupation used to assess qualifying skilled work experience
quiz.explanation: The primary occupation structures work history and eligibility.

quiz.question: What is a CRS cut-off score?
options: The minimum score invited in a particular draw
quiz.explanation: Each draw has its own cut-off and invitation criteria.

quiz.question: What are police certificates used for?
quiz.explanation: Police certificates support admissibility assessment.

quiz.question: What are immigration medical exams used for?
quiz.explanation: IRCC uses medical exams to assess health admissibility.

quiz.question: What is an enhanced PNP stream?
options: A provincial stream aligned with Express Entry
quiz.explanation: Enhanced nominations connect with Express Entry and can add CRS points.

quiz.question: What does entering the pool not create by itself?
quiz.explanation: Entering the pool is not the same as being invited or approved.

quiz.question: Why keep an Express Entry profile accurate?
options: Unsupported information can cause refusal or misrepresentation concerns
quiz.explanation: The PR application must support the profile's claims.

quiz.question: Which document is needed for foreign education CRS points?
quiz.explanation: An ECA is commonly required for points for foreign education.

quiz.question: What should an employment reference letter include?
options: Duties, title, dates, hours, salary, and employer details
quiz.explanation: Detailed letters help prove skilled work and NOC alignment.

quiz.question: Why are duties more important than title alone?
options: NOC matching is based mainly on duties
quiz.explanation: Officers compare actual duties to the claimed NOC.

quiz.question: What if circumstances change after ITA?
options: The application must remain accurate and eligibility must still be met
quiz.explanation: Post-ITA changes can affect CRS, eligibility, and documents.

quiz.question: What deadline follows an ITA?
options: A fixed deadline to submit the electronic PR application
quiz.explanation: The account shows the submission deadline.

quiz.question: What should proof of funds letters show?
quiz.explanation: Funds must satisfy IRCC settlement-fund requirements.

quiz.question: Who may not need settlement funds?
options: A CEC applicant or applicant with qualifying arranged employment where rules allow
quiz.explanation: Proof-of-funds exemptions depend on program and employment context.

quiz.question: What is arranged employment for CRS?
options: A qualifying valid job offer meeting Express Entry rules
quiz.explanation: Not every job offer qualifies for CRS points.

quiz.question: Why might an LMIA matter for job offer points?
options: Some offers need LMIA support unless an exemption applies
quiz.explanation: Valid job offer rules can require LMIA or qualifying LMIA-exempt status.

quiz.question: How is Canadian work counted for CEC?
options: Authorized paid skilled work in Canada
quiz.explanation: CEC requires qualifying Canadian skilled work gained with authorization.

quiz.question: Which work is excluded from CEC experience?
quiz.explanation: Unauthorized work cannot be used as qualifying Canadian experience.

quiz.question: How can part-time work count?
options: As equivalent experience if it meets program rules
quiz.explanation: Part-time hours can count when properly calculated.

quiz.question: Why calculate dates precisely?
options: Claiming experience too early can cause refusal
quiz.explanation: Eligibility often depends on exact dates, months, and hours.

quiz.question: How can a spouse's language test affect CRS?
options: It may add points for an accompanying spouse
quiz.explanation: Spousal factors can change CRS.

quiz.question: What should be done if a score is inflated before accepting ITA?
options: Correct or decline if points cannot be supported
quiz.explanation: Unsupported CRS claims can cause refusal.

quiz.question: What proves identity and travel history basics?
quiz.explanation: Passports support identity, status history, and travel history.

quiz.question: Why do police certificate country rules matter?
options: Requirements depend on where and how long the applicant lived
quiz.explanation: Applicants must follow country-specific instructions.

quiz.question: What is personal history used for?
options: Accounting for activities without unexplained gaps
quiz.explanation: Personal history supports background and consistency review.

quiz.question: What is a medical instruction process?
options: A way to complete the required immigration medical exam
quiz.explanation: Medical exams are part of PR admissibility screening.

quiz.question: Which fees are common at PR submission?
options: Processing fee and right of permanent residence fee
quiz.explanation: Express Entry PR applications include government fees.

quiz.question: Why prepare translations properly?
options: Non-English or non-French documents need acceptable translations
quiz.explanation: Translations must meet IRCC document standards.

quiz.question: What is a letter of explanation best for?
options: Clarifying specific document issues or unusual facts
quiz.explanation: It should clarify evidence, not replace eligibility.

quiz.question: What should be checked after new language results?
options: Whether CRS and program eligibility changed
quiz.explanation: Language results can affect thresholds and ranking.

quiz.question: Why do PNP nomination conditions matter?
options: The applicant may need to maintain intent and truthful provincial information
quiz.explanation: Federal review continues after nomination.

quiz.question: How should non-accompanying family be handled?
options: Disclose them and complete required examinations unless exempted
quiz.explanation: Family members must be disclosed even if they are not immigrating.

quiz.question: What if CRS drops below the draw cut-off after ITA?
options: The application may be refused if invitation criteria are no longer met
quiz.explanation: Applicants must support the score that justified the invitation.

quiz.question: Why is the lock-in date important?
options: Some facts are assessed at receipt while others must remain true
quiz.explanation: Timing affects age, documents, eligibility, and admissibility analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common work-experience misrepresentation risk?
options: Inflating duties, dates, hours, or salary
quiz.explanation: Unsupported work claims can lead to refusal and inadmissibility.

quiz.question: Why is self-employment complicated?
options: Counting rules and evidence standards differ by program
quiz.explanation: Self-employment may count differently for foreign experience and CEC.

quiz.question: What is the risk of choosing a NOC by title only?
options: Duties may not substantially match the claimed NOC
quiz.explanation: NOC alignment depends on actual duties.

quiz.question: What can make a job offer invalid for CRS points?
options: Failure to meet duration, employer, LMIA, exemption, or occupation rules
quiz.explanation: A valid CRS job offer has specific requirements.

quiz.question: Why disclose all prior refusals?
options: Failure to disclose can be misrepresentation
quiz.explanation: Immigration history must be truthful and complete.

quiz.question: What is the risk of personal-history gaps?
options: They can trigger background questions or document requests
quiz.explanation: Complete timelines support consistency and admissibility review.

quiz.question: How can proof of funds become non-compliant?
options: Funds are borrowed, encumbered, unavailable, or too low
quiz.explanation: Settlement funds must be genuinely available.

quiz.question: Why can a PNP nominee still be refused federally?
options: Federal officers still assess admissibility, eligibility, and truthfulness
quiz.explanation: Nomination does not eliminate federal review.

quiz.question: What if language results expire before eAPR submission?
options: They cannot support the application if no longer valid
quiz.explanation: Language tests must be valid when required.

quiz.question: Why is a coworker-only reference letter risky?
options: It may carry less weight than employer-issued evidence
quiz.explanation: Alternative evidence needs strong explanation and corroboration.

quiz.question: Why plan police certificates early?
options: Some countries take longer or have specific validity rules
quiz.explanation: Country-specific instructions can affect timing.

quiz.question: When might a procedural fairness response be needed?
options: When an officer doubts authenticity or work experience
quiz.explanation: A PFL allows response to serious concerns.

quiz.question: What is safest for a borderline NOC claim?
options: Choose the NOC best supported by duties and evidence
quiz.explanation: The strongest NOC is the one the records support.

quiz.question: Why track marital-status changes?
options: They can affect CRS, forms, family admissibility, and documents
quiz.explanation: Family composition changes must be disclosed.

quiz.question: What is a risk with Canadian work gained as a full-time student?
options: Some student work may be excluded from CEC eligibility
quiz.explanation: CEC has specific exclusions for student work.

quiz.question: Why do payroll records strengthen work evidence?
options: They corroborate paid employment, hours, and dates
quiz.explanation: Pay records support the genuineness and duration of employment.

quiz.question: What is the risk of missing the eAPR deadline?
options: Late or missing documents may lead to rejection or refusal
quiz.explanation: Express Entry deadlines are strict.

quiz.question: Why disclose non-accompanying dependants?
options: Undisclosed family can create future sponsorship and admissibility problems
quiz.explanation: All family members must be disclosed as required.

quiz.question: What if claimed CRS points cannot be proven?
quiz.explanation: The applicant must prove eligibility and claimed points.

quiz.question: Why do category-based draws require care?
options: The candidate must meet the specific category criteria
quiz.explanation: Category-based invitations rely on additional attributes.

quiz.question: What if arranged employment ends after ITA?
options: Job offer points may no longer be valid
quiz.explanation: Job offer points depend on continuing qualifying circumstances.

quiz.question: How should unobtainable documents be handled?
options: Provide proof of attempts, explanation, and acceptable alternatives if allowed
quiz.explanation: Diligent efforts should be documented.

quiz.question: Why can an old immigration violation affect Express Entry?
options: It may raise admissibility or credibility concerns
quiz.explanation: Past compliance can remain relevant.

donts: Do not guarantee ITA or PR
Do not collect full PR fee before ITA
Do not use expired language tests
Do not submit wrong NOC code
mistakes: Proof of funds not ready at ITA deadline

sampleDocs.title: Sample passport bio page (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Show clients which pages to scan — MRZ visible, no glare, full spread.

sampleDocs.title: Sample language test TRF (mock)
sampleDocs.description: IELTS / PTE / TOEFL layout — explain CLB or points mapping.

sampleDocs.title: Sample ECA report (mock)
sampleDocs.description: WES / IQAS / ICAS credential assessment — degree equivalency page.

sampleDocs.title: Sample employment reference letter (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Duties, NOC code, dates, and full-time hours on company letterhead.

sampleDocs.title: Sample resume / CV (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Skilled migration format — chronological roles and NOC alignment.

sampleDocs.title: Sample nomination / job offer letter (mock)
sampleDocs.description: PNP nomination or employer-backed offer where applicable.

sampleDocs.title: Sample bank statement (mock)
sampleDocs.description: 6-month statement with name, account number, and closing balance — explain seasoning.
